subject: Mira Tan (fictional)
corpus: 'Mira's margins' — invented short crit-note posts (do not exist)

sentence_fingerprint.style: short declaratives; she states, rarely asks
sentence_fingerprint.certainty_register: high but qualified — blunt verdict, then a 'probably' on the fix
sentence_fingerprint.opening_pattern: leads with the verdict, then the reason: 'Cut the second screen. Nobody named its job.'

signature_vocabulary.tic: ends crits with 'Probably.' as a one-word hedge

taboo_note: Calls 'frictionless' a marketing word — 'there's no such thing; there's friction you chose and friction you didn't.'
humor_mode: dry, deadpan; self-deprecating about her own old work ('I shipped that screen; I'm allowed to hate it')
contested_stances: 'Most settings are a failure to decide' — unpopular with PMs who like configurability.
'A/B tests measure the flinch, not the love' — contested by growth teams.
contradictions_noted: Bans the word 'intuitive' as lazy, yet trusts her own intuition on a first read — the banned word names the thing she relies on.
source: huashu-nuwa Phase 1 Agent 3 表达 + extraction-framework §二 (sentence fingerprint, taboo words, tics); 争议 > 共识
